open_warns.claim: This leg's two regression suites cannot pass on the CI Gate until the juggler-mcp dependency-install gap (999.1113) is closed, so the Gate cannot witness the fix as green. Deferring 999.1113 is defensible (separate infra layer, no new regression) but the proof-of-fix is only observable where juggler-mcp deps are installed.

juggler-backend's unit tests reach ACROSS the package boundary into the sibling juggler-mcp package (its source AND its node_modules) to require + mock the sibling's module. This inherent cross-boundary reach is the root cause of the CI dep-install coupling in ARCH-W1.

The two sibling packages float on divergent, stale SDK ranges (juggler-backend ^1.27.1 -> 1.29.0; juggler-mcp ^1.12.0 -> 1.27.1). The separate-package split is intentional and documented; the version DRIFT is not, and it is precisely this 'two packages on different SDK versions' condition that created the false premise behind a7d7dd1.
